toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.description: Run tests with crash dump collection enabled

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Run tests with crash dump collection enabled

data.reasoningText:
This is a .NET 9 MSTest project. The user wants to:
1. Run the tests
2. Collect a crash dump if any test crashes the host process

For .NET, you can collect crash dumps on crash using environment variables:
- `DOTNET_DbgEnableMiniDump=1` — enables mini dump collection on crash
- `DOTNET_DbgMiniDumpType=4` — full dump (1=Mini, 2=Heap, 3=Triage, 4=Full)
- `DOTNET_DbgMiniDumpName=<path>` — where to write the dump


- `DOTNET_DbgMiniDumpDir=<path>` — directory for dumps

Now I'll set up the environment variables and execute the test run.

arguments.description: Run tests with crash dump collection enabled

toolCalls.name: bash
toolCalls.args: {"command": "cd /tmp/sv-010896160b334926b81f6c66d5ed7adb && \\\n  DOTNET_DbgEnableMiniDump=1 \\\n  DOTNET_DbgMiniDumpType=4 \\\n  DOTNET_DbgMiniDumpName=/tmp/sv-010896160b334926b81f6c66d5ed7adb/crashdump_%p.dmp \\\n  dotnet test --no-pager 2>&1", "description": "Run tests with crash dump collection enabled", "initial_wait": 60}

toolArgs.description: Run tests with crash dump collection enabled

data.content:
All 3 tests passed. Here's what I ran:

```
DOTNET_DbgEnableMiniDump=1 \
DOTNET_DbgMiniDumpType=4 \
DOTNET_DbgMiniDumpName=./crashdump_%p.dmp \
dotnet test
```

**How it works:**
- `DOTNET_DbgEnableMiniDump=1` — tells the .NET runtime to write a dump on unhandled crash
- `DOTNET_DbgMiniDumpType=4` — captures a full dump (use `1` for a smaller mini dump)
- `DOTNET_DbgMiniDumpName` — output path (`%p` expands to the crashing process ID)

If a test crashes the host process, a `.dmp` file will be written automatically. No crash occurred this time, so no dump was generated.
